name: Puerto del Castillo - Fuensanta de
name_original: Castillo de Locubín vuelta por Fuensanta de Martos
description: A difficult 34.4-mile road cycling route in Jaén, offering 3677 feet of climbing and spectacular views of Andalusian white villages.
region_intro: Road cycling around Castillo De Locubín, located in the Sierra Sur of Jaén, Andalusia, features a rugged landscape characterized by extensive olive groves and a Mediterranean ecosystem. The region offers quiet, scenic roads with significant elevation gains, providing challenging climbs for road cyclists. This topography includes varied terrain suitable for demanding routes. Best road cycling routes around Castillo De Locubín The most popular road cycling route is Puerto del Castillo - Fuensanta de, a 34.4 miles (55.3 km) difficult trail that takes 3 hours 1 minute to complete. This route offers challenging ascents through the region's characteristic olive groves. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Puerto del Castillo – La Pandera loop from Castillo de Locubín, a difficult 47.7 miles (76.7 km) path. This route features substantial elevation gain across rugged landscapes. Local road cyclists also love the Puerto del Castillo – View of Alcalá la Real loop from Castillo de Locubín, a 29.5 miles (47.5 km) trail leading through varied terrain with views towards historical sites, often completed in about 2 hours 46 minutes. Road cycling around Castillo De Locubín is defined by extensive olive groves, rugged terrain, and challenging climbs. The network offers options for different ability levels, though many routes feature significant ascents. The routes in Castillo De Locubín are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 1000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Castillo De Locubín's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Castillo De Locubín?
A: There are over 15 road cycling routes around Castillo De Locubín, offering a variety of experiences for cyclists. These routes range from moderate to difficult, with a strong emphasis on challenging climbs and scenic landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Castillo De Locubín?
A: Road cycling in Castillo De Locubín features a rugged landscape characterized by extensive olive groves and a Mediterranean ecosystem. You can expect quiet, scenic roads with significant elevation gains, providing challenging climbs across varied terrain.

Q: Are there challenging climbs for road cyclists in Castillo De Locubín?
A: Yes, the region is known for its challenging ascents. Many routes feature significant elevation gains, with a general rule of thumb in the Andalusian hills being approximately 1,000 meters of climbing for every 50 kilometers. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40368009" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto del Castillo – La Pandera loop from Castillo de Locubín&lt;/a&gt; include over 2,000 meters of ascent.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes for beginners in Castillo De Locubín?
A: While many routes in Castillo De Locubín feature significant ascents and are rated moderate to difficult, the region's diverse network offers options for different ability levels. For a less strenuous experience, consider exploring sections of the Vía Verde del Aceite, which is a converted railway track offering flatter terrain, though it may require traveling a short distance from Castillo De Locubín to access.

Q: What landmarks or points of interest can I see along road cycling routes in Castillo De Locubín?
A: Many routes offer scenic viewpoints and pass near natural and historical attractions. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19381078" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto del Castillo – View of Alcalá la Real loop from Castillo de Locubín&lt;/a&gt; provides views towards historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5336873" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fortaleza de la Mota (Castle of Alcalá la Real)&lt;/a&gt;. Other routes might offer glimpses of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2379252" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the San Juan River&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3229479" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Marcos Rock Sculpture Forest&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there historical sites near the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. You can find 'pueblo blanco' (white villages) like Castillo De Locubín itself, offering a charming cultural experience. Nearby historical sites include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5336873" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fortaleza de la Mota (Castle of Alcalá la Real)&lt;/a&gt; and the castle in Alcaudete, which can be incorporated into longer rides such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33791139" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto del Castillo – View of Alcaudete Castle loop from Castillo de Locubín&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Castillo De Locubín?
A: Yes, several popular road cycling routes in Castillo De Locubín are circular. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40368009" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto del Castillo – La Pandera loop from Castillo de Locubín&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19381078" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto del Castillo – View of Alcalá la Real loop from Castillo de Locubín&lt;/a&gt;, allowing you to start and end in the same location.

Q: Can I access the Vía Verde del Aceite from Castillo De Locubín?
A: The Vía Verde del Aceite, a fantastic cycling route converted from an old railway track, is accessible from the region. While not directly starting in Castillo De Locubín, it's a notable cycling attraction nearby that offers stunning landscapes and a different cycling experience compared to the hilly road routes.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Castillo De Locubín?
A: The Mediterranean climate of Andalusia generally offers pleasant conditions for cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, providing milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summers can be hot, especially in July and August, so early morning rides are recommended during these months.

Q: Where can I find parking for cyclists in Castillo De Locubín?
A: Castillo De Locubín, as a 'pueblo blanco,' typically offers various public parking options within or near the town center. While specific dedicated cyclist parking isn't detailed, general public parking areas are usually available for visitors looking to explore the area by bike.

Q: Is public transport available to Castillo De Locubín for cyclists with bikes?
A: Public transport options to smaller towns like Castillo De Locubín, especially with bikes, can be limited. It's advisable to check local bus services (like ALSA) for their bike carriage policies and schedules in advance, as space for bicycles is often restricted or requires prior booking.

Q: Are there bike-friendly accommodations or places to eat in Castillo De Locubín?
A: Castillo De Locubín and its surrounding villages offer a selection of local cafes, restaurants, and accommodations. Many establishments are accustomed to outdoor enthusiasts, including cyclists. It's recommended to check with specific hotels or guesthouses regarding secure bike storage facilities when booking.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Castillo De Locubín?
A: The road cycling experience in Castillo De Locubín is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the quiet, scenic roads, the challenging climbs through extensive olive groves, and the rewarding views of the rugged Andalusian landscape.
tips: Small town on the JA-3055 road south of Martos. An 11 km climb towards La Pandera begins in Fuensanta de Martos. Don't forget to bring water.
A small town on top of a mountain in the southeast of the province of Jaén, near the border with Córdoba. Magnificent views of olive groves across the rugged landscape of the Sierras de Alcaudete.

faqs.question: How difficult is the Castillo de Locubin return via Fuensanta de Martos road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is considered difficult for road cycling. It features significant elevation changes and considerable climbing, typical of the mountainous Jaén province. You should expect a demanding profile, requiring very good fitness to tackle the ascents.

faqs.question: What scenic points or landmarks can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers spectacular views of the region. You'll pass through picturesque 'white villages' like Castillo de Locubín and Fuensanta de Martos. Notable highlights include the View of Fuensanta de Martos and the area around Puerto del Castillo. The trail also showcases diverse landscapes, from olive groves and cherry orchards to mountain ranges like Sierra Ahillos, providing expansive regional views.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is generally during the spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) months. During these periods, the weather is typically milder and more pleasant for demanding rides, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter conditions of winter.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the route in Castillo de Locubín or Fuensanta de Martos?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, both Castillo de Locubín and Fuensanta de Martos are small towns. You can typically find street parking in or near the town centers. It's advisable to look for public parking spaces or areas that are not restricted, especially near the start of the route in Castillo de Locubín.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to cycle this road route. It uses public roads and is freely accessible.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for dogs?
faqs.answer: This route is primarily on public roads and is designed for road cycling. While dogs are generally allowed in public spaces, the demanding nature of the ride and the focus on road cycling make it less suitable for bringing dogs along, especially if they are not accustomed to long distances or being carried in a specific bike trailer.

faqs.question: What villages does the route pass through?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Castillo de Locubín and includes Fuensanta de Martos. It also passes through areas like Hoyo de Piedra and Las Casillas de Martos, offering a glimpse into several charming Andalusian 'white villages'.

faqs.question: Is this a loop route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the name 'Castillo de Locubin return via Fuensanta de Martos' suggests, this is a loop route. You will start and finish in Castillo de Locubín, making a return journey through Fuensanta de Martos.

faqs.question: Are there any less challenging cycling options nearby for beginners?
faqs.answer: While this specific route is challenging, the wider Jaén area offers other cycling opportunities. Notably, the 'Vía Verde del Aceite' (Oil Greenway) is a converted old railway line that provides flatter, scenic routes through olive groves, which could be a good option for beginners or those looking for a less strenuous ride.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this challenging road cycling ride?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and significant climbing, you should bring plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit (including spare tubes and a pump), appropriate cycling gear, sun protection, and a fully charged phone. It's also wise to carry a basic first-aid kit and be prepared for varying weather conditions.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through towns like Castillo de Locubín and Fuensanta de Martos, you will find opportunities for cafes, bars, and restaurants to refuel. These villages are characteristic of Andalusia and offer local hospitality.
